content.overview: This project aims to implement a high-performance, event-driven data synchronization pipeline to bridge the gap between RetailMax's PostgreSQL-based Order Management System and Salesforce Sales Cloud. By leveraging Change Data Capture (CDC) via Debezium and AWS SQS, the system will provide real-time updates for critical B2B metrics (B2B_Order_Total__c and Last_Order_Date__c) while avoiding the production database locking issues encountered in previous ETL attempts. The project also includes a high-throughput historical backfill engine to migrate 3 million records within a strict 3-hour maintenance window.

success_metrics.before: Inconsistent latency often exceeding several hours or failing entirely.
success_metrics.after: Consistent end-to-end latency of less than 5 minutes from database commit to Salesforce visibility.

success_metrics.before: Previous attempts resulted in production timeouts or exceeded maintenance windows.
success_metrics.after: Successful ingestion of 3,000,000 records within a 3-hour window (~277 records/sec).

success_metrics.before: High CPU spikes and table-level locking during data extraction.
success_metrics.after: Logical replication overhead strictly maintained below 10% of total RDS CPU/IOPS capacity.

business_goals: Establish a high-performance, event-driven data pipeline to synchronize critical B2B order metrics from PostgreSQL to Salesforce.
Execute a massive historical backfill of 3 million records within a 3-hour maintenance window without disrupting operations.
Eliminate production database performance risks by transitioning to a decoupled CDC architecture using Debezium and AWS SQS.
Improve operational reliability through a robust error-handling framework utilizing Dead Letter Queues (DLQ) and Slack alerting.

users.pain: Lack of real-time overview of technical progress and infrastructure bottlenecks.
users.goal: Ensure infrastructure reliability and project delivery within the 6-week mandate.

users.pain: Difficulty in diagnosing performance bottlenecks in real-time data flows.
users.goal: Meet the 5-minute latency SLA and ensure data integrity across systems.

users.pain: Operational complexities related to RDS logical replication and WAL management.
users.goal: Maintain robust RDS and Debezium infrastructure on AWS ECS.

out_of_scope: Synchronization of order data older than 12 months.
Modifications to core Shopify storefront logic.
Automated retry logic with exponential backoff.
Deployment of Kafka or Kubernetes (strictly ECS/SQS only).

in_scope.description: Real-time Change Data Capture from PostgreSQL WAL logs using Debezium.
Salesforce synchronization service for B2B_Order_Total__c and Last_Order_Date__c.
High-throughput historical backfill engine for 3M records.
Dead Letter Queue (DLQ) for manual exception handling of failed records.

non_functional.requirement: End-to-end data latency from PostgreSQL commit to Salesforce visibility must be under 5 minutes.
Use SQS FIFO queues to guarantee message ordering and prevent stale data updates in Salesforce.
The pipeline must handle a 2x increase in CDC volume over 6 months without architectural changes.

features.description: Implement Debezium Standalone on AWS ECS to monitor PostgreSQL Write-Ahead Logs (WAL). Capture INSERT/UPDATE events for B2B orders and stream them as JSON to AWS SQS. Logical replication overhead must remain below 10% of RDS capacity.
features.title: Real-time Change Data Capture (CDC) via Debezium

features.description: An ECS-based service to consume SQS events and update 'B2B_Order_Total__c' and 'Last_Order_Date__c' in Salesforce using Bulk API v2. Must maintain < 5-minute end-to-end latency.
features.title: Salesforce Metric Synchronization Service

features.description: Automatically create a new Salesforce Account record if a Shopify order is associated with a customer ID that does not exist in the CRM, using predefined attributes (Email, Name, Shopify ID).
features.title: Automated Salesforce Account Provisioning

features.description: A specialized script to process 3,000,000 records within a 3-hour Sunday window. Must include throttling to respect Salesforce Bulk API v2 limits and prevent 429 errors.
features.title: High-Throughput Historical Backfill Engine

features.description: A configuration mode for the backfill engine that performs extraction and mapping validation against Salesforce schema without committing the final API call.
features.title: Backfill Simulation (Dry-Run) Mode

features.description: Route failed synchronization records to an SQS DLQ with full error metadata. This supports the requirement for manual review by the lean engineering team.
features.title: Dead Letter Queue (DLQ) for Manual Exception Handling

features.description: Trigger Slack notifications only after 5 consecutive failures for the same Salesforce Account ID to minimize alert fatigue while escalating persistent issues.
features.title: Intelligent Slack Alerting with Failure Thresholds

features.description: Configure CloudWatch dashboards to track SQS Queue Depth and Debezium Replication Lag, ensuring visibility into the 5-minute SLA compliance.
features.title: Operational Health Monitoring via CloudWatch

open_questions: What is the exact daily Bulk API batch limit currently consumed by other Salesforce integrations?
Can we obtain a temporary IAM role with RDS:ModifyDBInstance permissions to bypass the account owner bottleneck?
What is the specific data type and validation logic for B2B_Order_Total__c on the Salesforce side?

risks.description: Potential for WAL bloat on RDS if Debezium stops consuming, leading to storage exhaustion.
Exceeding Salesforce Bulk API v2 limits during the 3M record backfill burst.
Project delay due to AWS account owner absence blocking logical replication enablement.
